mechanism_narrative: A persistently low or falling federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present value of expected earnings and producing positive forward S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month regardless of level.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent indicating near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of periods with falling or low FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, equity markets price in accelerating labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as risk premia are quickly repriced.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following a >0.3pp UNRATE rise are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) across the sample, or the sign hit-rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a broad-based consumer demand expansion that jointly drives corporate revenue growth expectations upward, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transitory spending rather than durable expansion.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns conditional on joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration are not statistically different from unconditional baseline returns (p > 0.05), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals reaccelerating manufacturing output and corporate earnings capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; this relationship is regime-specific and weakens or inverts during late-cycle overheating when INDPRO gains raise inflation and tightening fears instead.
regime_hint: early-cycle recovery following labor market slack
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns is not positive and statistically distinguishable from zero specifically within early-cycle subsamples identified by high UNRATE combined with rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects durable labor income expansion that supports consumption and corporate revenue over a full quarter, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns at a 60-day horizon as an always-on trend signal independent of any threshold condition.
failure_condition: Correlation between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05 or statistically insignificant across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in CPIAUCSL above trend triggers immediate market repricing of future rate hike expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors front-run anticipated monetary tightening.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following a CPI upside surprise trigger are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears when controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS trend.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with concurrent retail sales (RSAFS) strength jointly confirms that aggregate output expansion is being validated by end-consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; GDP growth alone is noisy and lagged, so the interaction with RSAFS is required to filter out data revisions that do not reflect real-time demand conditions.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1-RSAFS trigger produces 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional distribution, or GDPC1 revisions materially reverse the sign of the trigger in more than 30% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime where FEDFUNDS is near 0% and UNRATE remains elevated above 6%, equity markets interpret continued labor market slack as assurance that policy will remain accommodative, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this relationship reverses once UNRATE falls below a level consistent with full employment, at which point continued low rates instead signal policy behind the curve.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09 percent and UNRATE at 6.1 percent
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during periods satisfying both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE conditions are not significantly positive versus baseline, or the relationship fails to reverse sign once UNRATE drops below 5% in out-of-sample data.
